titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Marguerite Moreno Papers
titlestmt.author: by Karen Spicher

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday October 26, 2018 at 04:16

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The Marguerite Moreno Papers consist of letters from Moreno to Gaston Baheux, an animateur of Parisian cabarets known as Tonton de Montmartre; other correspondence; journals, drafts of published writings, and unidentified writings; family photographs, photographs of Moreno, and theater and film production photographs of her and other actors; and a small amount of personal papers. The papers mainly concern Moreno's friendships and acting career, particularly during the 1930s-1940s, and her autobiographical writings, with some materials relating to her experiences as an acting teacher in Argentina and as a nurse during World War I.

langmaterial.language: Chiefly in French; some materials in Spanish.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Autographes Demarest on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2009.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Marguerite Moreno Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Marguerite Moreno Papers. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: A vendor description listing contents of some correspondence, writings, and other materials is filed in Box 1.

bioghist.head: Marguerite Moreno (1871-1948)
bioghist.p: Marguerite Moreno was a French theater and film actor. Born Lucie Marie Marguerite Monceau, she adopted her mother's name at the beginning of her career. Moreno was associated with Stéphane Mallarmé and the symbolist movement, and she was a member of the Comédie-Française, 1890-1903. During the 1890s she met the writer Colette, who became a friend and confidant. During 1908-1913, she taught at the conservatory in Buenos Aires, Argentina. Moreno served as a nurse in France during World War I, 1914-1915. By 1915 she was acting in films, and continued working in theater and film through the end of her life. In 1945, she performed the title role in Jean Giraudoux's La Folle de Chaillot.

Moreno was married to actors Marcel Schwob, 1900-1905, and Jean Daragon, 1908-1923. In the 1920s, she acquired La Source bleue, her home in Touzac (Lot), where she lived with her nephew and companion Pierre Bouyou, an actor who adopted the name Pierre Moreno.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Marguerite Moreno Papers consist of letters from Moreno to Gaston Baheux, an animateur of Parisian cabarets known as Tonton de Montmartre; other correspondence; journals, drafts of published writings, and unidentified writings; family photographs, photographs of Moreno, and theater and film production photographs of her and other actors; and a small amount of personal papers. The papers mainly concern Moreno's friendships and acting career, particularly during the 1930s-1940s, and her autobiographical writings, with some materials relating to her experiences as an acting teacher in Argentina and as a nurse during World War I.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into four series: I. Correspondence, 1890-1949, undated. II. Writings, 1914-1915, undated. III. Photographs, 1892-1946, undated. IV. Other Papers, 1882-1948, undated.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series I, Correspondence, is arranged alphabetically by correspondent, followed by third party correspondence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Most correspondence consists of letters from Moreno to Gaston Baheux. Circa 450 letters to Baheux are present, concerning their friendship, Moreno's work in theater and films during the 1930s-1940s, and other subjects including Colette, Moreno's home, in Touzac, her relationship with Pierre Moreno and his wife Micheline Moreno, and experiences during World War II.
Other correspondence includes letters to Moreno from Eugène Morand, concerning performances of Hamlet in a French translation by Morand and Marcel Schwob.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letter from Pierre Moreno to Baheux, announcing the death of Marguerite Moreno

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series II, Writings, is arranged alphabetically by title or genre, followed by unidentified writings and writings of others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Three published autobiographical works are represented by holograph drafts: Le bonhomme de neige , Origines du coeur , and Le statue de sel . Holograph drafts are also present for several short stories, as well as unidentified writings, and fragments. Journals for 1914-1915 document Moreno's nursing work in a military hospital in Nice. Writings of others consist mostly of unidentified poems and play fragments.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series III, Photographs, is arranged by subject.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs of Marguerite Moreno include portraits, theater and film production photographs, and family and other informal photographs. Theater and film production photographs consist of photographs of theater performances, film stills, and publicity images of Moreno and other actors, most unidentified.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Archival Materials note
relatedmaterial.p: See also: Other Papers/Passport and other identification

did.unittitle: Photographs of drawings and paintings of Moreno

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes one original painting by an unidentified artist

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Eddie Cantor, Constant Coquelin, [Edmond Rostand?], Jean-Dominique Van Caulaert, and groups with Marguerite Moreno

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes theater and film production photographs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Moreno's parents, her brothers, and groups with Marguerite Moreno

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes theater and film production photographs
Includes Micheline Moreno, other family members, and groups with Marguerite Moreno and unidentified people

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes groups with Marguerite Moreno and Jean Daragon

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes groups with Marguerite Moreno, Jean Daragon, Pierre Moreno

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Marguerite Moreno and unidentified people

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series IV, Other Papers, is arranged by type of material.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Materials include a scrapbook of clippings about performances by Moreno and André Paysan in Argentina, 1911; a few family legal documents; and drafts and correspondence concerning an unrealized project for management of the Théâtre Sarah Bernhardt, proposed by Moreno and Edmond Roze in 1923. Also present are several contracts for theater performances concerning Moreno, Marcel Schwob, Sarah Bernhardt, and Sacha Guitry; correspondence about other contracts is filed in the Correspondence Series.
